title: Lastrea paludosa
intro: Lastrea paludosa, also known as the marsh fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea paludosa include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lastrea paludosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Lastrea paludosa is classified as a species of fern in the genus Lastrea, which belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf shape and structure, and its reproductive features. The genus Lastrea is a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world.
